title: Tomophyllum bipinnatifidum
intro: Tomophyllum bipinnatifidum is a species of fern within the family Polypodiaceae, commonly found in tropical regions. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, skin infections, and respiratory problems, due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Tomophyllum bipinnatifidum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Tomophyllum bipinnatifidum is often used to prepare teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being used in topical applications such as creams and ointments.
taxonomy: Tomophyllum bipinnatifidum is a species of fern in the family Polypodiaceae. The genus Tomophyllum is a part of the family Polypodiaceae. Polypodiaceae is a family of ferns in the division Pteridophyta. The division Pteridophyta consists of vascular plants that are seedless and reproduce via spores. This classification places Tomophyllum bipinnatifidum within the broader group of vascular, seedless plants.
uses: Tomophyllum bipinnatifidum is used in traditional medicine due to its various health benefits.
